1.texto_original: Y apareció en el cielo una gran señal; una mujer vestida del sol, y la luna debajo de sus pies, y sobre su cabeza una corona de doce estrellas.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Revelación 12:1

"Y apareció en el cielo una gran señal; una mujer vestida del sol, y la luna debajo de sus pies, y sobre su cabeza una corona de doce estrellas."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Revelación 12:1

Philological Analysis

"Y apareció en el cielo una gran señal; una mujer vestida del sol, y la luna debajo de sus pies, y sobre su cabeza una corona de doce estrellas."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y apareció en el cielo una gran señal; una mujer vestida del sol, y la luna debajo de sus pies, y sobre su cabeza una corona de doce estrellas."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

"Y fueron dadas a la mujer dos alas de grande águila, para que de la presencia de la serpiente volase al desierto, a su lugar, donde es sustentada por un tiempo, y tiempos, y la mitad de un tiempo."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Y la serpiente echó de su boca, tras la mujer, agua como un río, a fin de hacer que fuese arrastrada por el río.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of Revelación 12:15

"Y la serpiente echó de su boca, tras la mujer, agua como un río, a fin de hacer que fuese arrastrada por el río."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Revelación 12:15

Philological Analysis

"Y la serpiente echó de su boca, tras la mujer, agua como un río, a fin de hacer que fuese arrastrada por el río."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

16.texto_original: Pero la tierra ayudó a la mujer, pues la tierra abrió su boca, y sorbió el río que el dragón había echado de su boca.
16.normal:
Devotional Study of Revelación 12:16

"Pero la tierra ayudó a la mujer, pues la tierra abrió su boca, y sorbió el río que el dragón había echado de su boca."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

17.texto_original: Entonces el dragón se enfureció contra la mujer; y se fue a hacer guerra contra el remanente de la simiente de ella, los cuales guardan los mandamientos de Dios, y tienen el testimonio de Jesucristo.
17.normal:
Devotional Study of Revelación 12:17

"Entonces el dragón se enfureció contra la mujer; y se fue a hacer guerra contra el remanente de la simiente de ella, los cuales guardan los mandamientos de Dios, y tienen el testimonio de Jesucristo."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
